message:
feat(abi): Add minimal host ABI layer for WASM sandbox isolation

Implement new ABI architecture where all complex logic runs in WASM:

New files:
- src/abi/host.zig: Minimal host imports (~13 functions)
  - random_get: Only crypto operation from host
  - net_connect/send/recv/close: Raw socket I/O
  - proc_spawn/wait/kill: Raw process management
  - fs_open/read/write/close/stat/readdir: Raw file I/O

- src/abi/crypto.zig: Crypto in WASM using std.crypto
  - hash (sha256/384/512, md5, sha1)
  - hmac (sha256/384/512, md5, sha1)
  - randomBytes (uses host_random_get)
  - randomUuid

- src/abi/mod.zig: ABI exports for JavaScript
  - Memory management (zig_alloc, zig_free)
  - Crypto (zig_hash, zig_hmac, zig_random_*)
  - Filesystem (zig_fs_read/write/exists/stat/readdir)
  - Process (zig_spawn_sync)
  - Utility (zig_base64_encode/decode)

- src/polyfills/zig_abi.js: Thin JS wrapper (~300 lines)
  - Memory helpers (encodeString, decodeResult)
  - Node.js shims (require('crypto'), require('fs'), etc.)
  - Simple pointer-based interface

Security benefits:
- HTTP parsing bugs crash WASM, not host
- Crypto operations isolated in sandbox
- Minimal attack surface (13 host functions vs 30+)
- One bad request cannot crash entire server
